oracle_b.reason: arm = maw@new-layout; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# maw arm crib (proposed consolidated `.maw/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is a normal checkout (with `.git/`) containing a hidden `.maw/` admin directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `.maw/workspaces/<name>/`. The default integration target is the repo root itself (no separate `ws/default/`). The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `.maw/workspaces/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --check` — dry-run a merge into the repo root; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into the repo root and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.maw/manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpTReESo
Scenario seed: 8161035977218302039

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. In workspace `ws-0`, edit the following files (any content is fine; the test cares about coordination): shared/file-1.txt, shared/file-3.txt, ws-0/file-0.txt
3. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-1` based on the project's default branch.
4. Commit pending changes in workspace `ws-0` with a descriptive message.

# maw arm crib (proposed consolidated `.maw/` layout)

The repo is a normal checkout (with `.git/`) containing a hidden `.maw/` admin directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `.maw/workspaces/<name>/`. ## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `.maw/workspaces/<name>/`.

- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.

- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

turns.reply_text: I'll start by inspecting the workspace and then complete the tasks systematically.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw doctor && maw ws list","description":"Check substrate health and list workspaces"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
maw doctor
==========

[OK] git: git version 2.54.0
[OK] git version: 2.54.0 (>= 2.40.0)
[OK] manifold metadata: .maw/manifold/ exists
[OK] default workspace: consolidated layout — root checkout at /tmp/.tmpTReESo
[OK] lfs: no LFS tracked patterns
[OK] repo root: consolidated layout (live checkout at root)
[OK] legacy jj metadata: none
[OK] dangling snapshots: none
[OK] abandoned-with-snapshot: no queued recovery snapshots
[OK] stale head refs: none
[OK] merge-state: no merge in progress
[OK] epoch drift: epoch ref not set (run `maw init`)
[OK] git HEAD: ref: refs/heads/main

All checks passed!
No workspaces found.

turns.reply_text: Substrate is healthy. Now I'll create workspace `ws-0`.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Workspace create requires an explicit source.
  Use one of:
    maw ws create --from main ws-0
    maw ws create --change <change-id> ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0 from main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Creating workspace 'ws-0' at .maw/workspaces/ws-0 ...

Workspace 'ws-0' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  fb34a974cc12 (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmpTReESo/.maw/workspaces/ws-0/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmpTReESo/.maw/workspaces/ws-0/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-0 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

turns.reply_text: Workspace `ws-0` created. Now I'll create the required files and directories in it.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"mkdir -p /tmp/.tmpTReESo/.maw/workspaces/ws-0/shared /tmp/.tmpTReESo/.maw/workspaces/ws-0/ws-0","description":"Create shared and ws-0 directories in workspace ws-0"}

tool_calls.name: Write

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpTReESo/.maw/workspaces/ws-0/shared/file-3.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpTReESo/.maw/workspaces/ws-0/ws-0/file-0.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

turns.reply_text: All three files created. Now create workspace `ws-1` from main in parallel with committing ws-0.

tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-1","description":"Create workspace ws-1 from main branch"}

To start working:

turns.reply_text: Now commit the pending changes in `ws-0`.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git add -A && maw exec ws-0 -- git status","description":"Stage all changes in ws-0 and check status"}

tool_calls.name: Bash

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks are complete:

1. **ws-0 created** from `main` — `maw ws create --from main ws-0`
2. **Files edited in ws-0** — `shared/file-1.txt`, `shared/file-3.txt`, `ws-0/file-0.txt` all written with coordination content
3. **ws-1 created** from `main` — `maw ws create --from main ws-1`
4. **ws-0 committed** — commit `42506bf` with message `feat(ws-0): add coordination files for shared and ws-0 directories`

Both workspaces are in `clean` lifecycle state with no orphaned work.
